description: This file contains Balanda future negative modal constructions using 'mamve'. This modality expresses future inability or impossibility and corresponds to the English meaning 'will not be able to'.

verb_form.structure: le-VERB (base verb form with future prefix)

affirmative_negative_form.notes: This construction is inherently negative in meaning

question_form.function: Forms yes/no questions expressing future inability

constraints.structure: mamve replaces nonve in negative future modality; le- must attach directly to the verb; The verb remains in its base form aside from the le- prefix; gba must appear at the end of the clause before the question particle; No additional tense or aspect markers may co-occur with le-; No word order change occurs in questions; we? always appears at the end of the clause

output: I will not be able to eat food.
I will not be able to drink water.
He will not be able to sleep.

input: Ne mamve leyai a Wau gba
output: I will not be able to go to Wau.

input: Ni mamve ledhii agbi tiyi gba
output: He will not be able to come to the house.

input: Wiki mamve lenono gbi difo gba
output: The child will not be able to walk in the compound.

input: Ni mamve lemande gbi njü gba
output: He will not be able to work in the garden.
We will not be able to play.
I will not be able to love you.
I will not be able to know the truth.
He will not be able to think.
I will not be able to ask them.

input: Ni mamve level e takoni gba
output: He will not be able to tell a story.
I will not be able to see you.
He will not be able to hear.
I will not be able to write a book.
He will not be able to read a book.
I will not be able to buy a bike.
He will not be able to sell food.
I will not be able to push the fruit.
He will not be able to pull the drum.
He will not be able to turn the table.

input: Ne mamve lekeye ti ndi gba
output: I will not be able to call them.
We will not be able to discuss the story.
He will not be able to hug the child.
They will not be able to fight.
He will not be able to protect the child.
He will not be able to fall.
He will not be able to change.
The tree will not be able to darken.
The school will not be able to develop.
The child will not be able to mature.

input: Ni mamve ledho gbi tiyi gba
output: He will not be able to be in the house.
I will not be able to do the work.

input: Ne mamve lewala gbi Wau gba
output: I will not be able to live in Wau.
I will not be able to want water.
He will not be able to remain behind.
He will not be able to sing.
He will not be able to dance.
I will not be able to watch the animals.

input: Ne mamve leja nza gba we?
output: Will I not be able to eat food?

output: Will I not be able to drink water?
